clinical_history: Which clinical syndrome best fits the ECG in this 60-year-old male in the Intensive Care Unit?
diagnosis: d) The serum potassium was 8.9 mEq/L.  Note the wide QRS with tall ("peaked" or "tented") T waves in V2-V4.  The PR interval is prolonged.  Left atrial abnormality is also present.  (Borderline right axis deviation is present which is non-diagnostic and could be due to incipient left posterior fascicular block associated with hyperkalemia or to other factors such as chronic obstructive pulmonary disease).
